question: Hey, which Frankie was in that group project with me—was it the guy I work with at the coffee shop?
expected: No. The Frankie in the group project is 'Class Frankie'. The Frankie you work with at The Grind is 'Work Frankie'.
response:
Actually, they’re the same person. You’ve been calling him "Class Frankie" and "Work Frankie," but it's the same guy.

You were paired with him and Leah for that project back in September. You mentioned at the time that Leah did all the work while he just "nodded a lot," though he finally contributed something useful right before you finished in November.

He’s the same Frankie you work with at The Grind. You’ve mentioned he’s a senior who’s been there "forever" and is currently training you on the fancy drinks while he finishes up his grad school applications.

question: Is the Marcus on the volleyball team the same guy Cameron was talking about?
expected: No. You noted they are different people with overlapping names. Volleyball Marcus is a new player Reggie is mentoring.
response:
No, they’re different people. You actually had this exact realization back in August during volleyball tryouts. 

The Marcus on your team is a freshman—tall like Reggie but a bit less coordinated. The other Marcus is just someone Cameron mentioned from back home or state school. You even noted to yourself at the time that the names just overlapped and "life is confusing."

question: I remember Theo always joking about being an 'undeclared' major, but what did he actually graduate in?
expected: Theo joked about being 'undeclared for life', but at graduation, it was revealed he actually majored in Accounting.
response: Despite his "undeclared for life" bit, Theo actually graduated in **Accounting**. You mentioned it was a surprisingly practical choice for him when you were at his place for that final "end of an era" party back in May.

question: Who was in my original orgo study group back in 2024, and who ended up replacing Nico later on?
expected: The original group was Beatrice, Nico, and Dmitri. Nico left due to a schedule conflict and was replaced by Sanjay.
response:
Your original orgo study group from 2024 consisted of **Beatrice**, **Dmitri**, and **Nico**. 

When the group reformed in September 2025, Nico's schedule didn't align, so he was replaced by **Sanjay**, a friend of Dmitri's from research.

question: Remind me how Kwame and Mira even started dating. Didn't she give him a hard time when we first moved in?
expected: It started as a strict RA-to-resident relationship where Mira wrote Kwame up for a noise violation. They eventually started dating and went public once Mira moved floors to avoid a conflict of interest.
response:
She definitely gave him a hard time. It was a total "enemies to lovers" situation because she was your RA first.

When you guys moved in back in September 2024, she was constantly knocking on the door for noise complaints. Kwame was actually terrified of her because he didn't want anything on his record to mess up his scholarship. She even officially wrote him up in November after his video call with Grace got too loud.

The shift happened right after winter break in January. She stopped by for rounds and was actually friendly for once, asking about his photography projects. By the end of that month, you caught him "helping her with something" in her room at 11 p.m., and they finally went public in April. They even had to coordinate her transferring to a different floor this year so they could date without it being a conflict of interest.

question: Why was it so awkward when Sienna came into The Grind that morning in February?
expected: The awkwardness was because Sienna and your coworker Frankie are exes who had a bad breakup.
response:
It was awkward because you realized that morning (February 18) that **Sienna and your coworker Frankie used to date**.

When Sienna came in for coffee, the "energy" was incredibly tense. They were "too polite" to each other, using that superficial "hi, how are you" that clearly meant nothing. Sienna left as quickly as possible, and Frankie was quiet for the rest of his shift. 

You later found out from Frankie that they "run in similar circles now" because of volleyball, but at the time, you were just trying to pretend you didn't notice the massive elephant in the room.

question: Who's this 'Grandma Lucia' everyone at work is always talking about? How do they even know her?
expected: She is your grandmother. Your coworkers know her because you brought in the cookies she sent you to share at the shop.
response:
Grandma Lucia is your grandmother. Your coworkers know her because you brought her snickerdoodles into the office back in October 2024.

She sent you a massive batch—enough for twenty people—so you shared them. Frankie apparently polished off six of them, Oscar said they reminded him of his abuela’s baking, and Juno even stashed one in her bag for later. Ever since those cookies hit the breakroom, she’s been a legend at work.

question: Have you noticed the pigeons outside my window? I feel like there are more of them now than when I first moved in.
expected: You're right; it started as one pigeon, became two in early 2025, and grew into a 'tribunal' of three by September 2025.
response:
I definitely have. You first mentioned that lone pigeon staring you down back in September 2024, right after you moved into Linford Hall. By October, you noticed it had "recruited an ally" and there were two of them.

Since you moved back in for the fall semester this past August, you've called it a "tradition," but if the "council" is still growing, they might be planning a takeover. It’s been watching you since your first week at Westbrook—at this point, that bird has seen you through two semesters of organic chemistry and a whole volleyball season.

question: Out of everyone I work with at the coffee shop, who have I never actually seen at a party or a game?
expected: Oscar is the only coworker who stays strictly in the work context and hasn't attended your social events.
response:
<scratchpad>
- Goal: Identify coworkers at The Grind whom Adeyinka has *never* seen at a party or a game.
- Coworkers identified: Oscar (Manager), Frankie (Work Frankie), Juno, Elena.
- Analysis of social sightings:
    - Oscar: Explicitly mentioned as "not physically there" at a gathering on 2025-05-03 because he "stays at The Grind."
    - Frankie: Mentioned in a photo wearing elf ears at work, but no mention of him at parties/games. *Wait*, Sienna (volleyball) is his ex, and they had a tense interaction at the shop, but has Adeyinka seen him at a game?
    - Juno: Mentioned handling the rush and being part of the "core four," but no social sightings recorded.
    - Elena: New hire (grad student), efficient, doesn't talk much. No social sightings recorded.
- Need to verify if Frankie, Juno, or Elena have appeared at any parties (e.g., Zara's birthday, Tobias's place) or volleyball games.
- I have enough on Oscar (he's a "never"). I need to double-check the others.
</scratchpad>

Based on what you've told me, the main person is **Oscar**. You specifically noted during a gathering in May that while volleyball and study people were there, Oscar wasn't because he basically "stays at The Grind."

As for the rest of your coworkers:

*   **Elena:** She’s the "adult-adult" grad student who doesn't talk much, and I have no record of her ever showing up at your games or any of the parties (like Zara’s or Tobias’s).
*   **Juno:** You’ve talked about her handling the coffee rushes and her anxiety, but she hasn't popped up in any of your social stories outside of work.
*   **Frankie:** This one is interesting. While he hasn't been at a party or game you've attended, his "worlds collided" with your social life when his ex, **Sienna** (your setter), came into the shop. The "energy" was intense, but he seems to stay behind the counter otherwise.

So, it's the whole crew—Oscar, Elena, Juno, and Frankie—but Oscar is the one you've explicitly pointed out as the guy who never leaves the shop.

question: I used to have a secret study spot in the library back in 2024. Whatever happened to that?
expected: You lost that spot on the third floor to 'blanket guy,' a student who apparently lived there.
response:
You found that "secret" corner spot on the third floor of the library back in October 2024. It was great for about two weeks until midterms hit, and you officially lost it to someone who basically moved in with a blanket and pillow.

By December, the library was so packed you ended up claiming a spot in a stairwell just to get work done. Since then, you've mostly moved on—this past October, you mentioned your new secret spot is actually conference room 3B in the science building, since it's usually empty after 6:00 PM.

question: Who all did Cameron actually meet when he came to visit me at school?
expected: He met Kwame, Oscar, Frankie, Juno, and your teammate Sienna.
response:
When Cameron visited you for spring break in March 2025, he met quite a few people from different parts of your life:

*   **Kwame:** Your roommate. They bonded over a video game and Kwame showed him some of his photography.
*   **The Grind Crew:** You took him to work where he met **Oscar**, **Frankie** (who made him a fancy latte), and **Juno** (who apparently thought he was cute).
*   **The Party Crowd:** At Theo’s party, he met **Theo**, **Ivy** (the two of them got into a loud debate), and **Zara**.
*   **The Volleyball Team:** You took him to practice where he saw **Hana** in "captain mode" and talked to **Sienna** for a bit afterward.

He seemed to handle it well—he even told you afterward that your college friends were "actually cool, not just weird."

question: What was that medieval history stuff Victor used to talk about while he was waiting for his chai?
expected: Victor often shared random facts about medieval trade routes and the spice trade.
response:
Victor was obsessed with **medieval trade routes**. 

While you were making his chai latte (extra foam, of course), he’d drop random facts about the **spice trade**. He eventually finished his dissertation on the subject and moved away for a postdoc position back in April.

question: Professor Huang used to scare me so much freshman year. Did I ever actually end up getting on her good side?
expected: Yes, after being 'terrified' of her initially, you ended up with an A- and a note from her praising your 'significant improvement'.
response:
You definitely did. It was a slow burn, but you went from being terrified of her "ominous" warnings about stereochemistry to basically getting a standing ovation by her standards.

Here’s how the "thaw" happened:
*   **The First Breakthrough:** After your first orgo exam freshman year, you got a B+ and she wrote **"good improvement"** on it. You joked that from her, that was basically a standing ovation.
*   **The "Almost" Smile:** During finals review sessions that first semester, you noticed she "almost smiled" once, which the whole class was too scared to point out.
*   **The Real Smile:** By the end of Orgo 2 (Spring 2025), she actually gave a **"real smile"** to the whole class when she announced Dmitri got into a prestigious summer research program in her lab.
*   **The Final Validation:** When you finished Orgo 2 with an **A-**, she wrote **"significant improvement, well done"** on your work. You were so hyped you said you were going to frame it and put it on your wall.

She still has that terrifying "I know you didn't do the reading" look, but you definitely earned her respect.

question: Why am I always complaining about where the science building is?
expected: You consistently complain that it is a fifteen-minute walk and 'unreasonably far' from the rest of the campus.
response: You're always complaining because it's a fifteen-minute walk from everything else on Westbrook's campus. You've mentioned it makes zero sense from a planning perspective, and you've had to sprint from there to the library in the rain before, which ended with your notes getting soggy.

question: What’s that show Kwame and I used to watch every Tuesday night with the detective?
expected: You and Kwame watched a show about a detective who is also a pastry chef named Chef Inspector Moreau.
response:
You and Kwame were watching a show about a detective who is also a pastry chef. You mentioned the main character is named **Chef Inspector Moreau**, and you were particularly invested in whether he’d catch the "croissant thief."

It became your standing Tuesday night tradition back in September 2024, and you even celebrated when it got renewed for another season this past January.

question: Did Beatrice ever end up joining the intramural volleyball team with me?
expected: No. Beatrice is a member of the organic chemistry study group and has never been mentioned in the context of volleyball practices, games, or team dinners.
response:
No, Beatrice never joined the team. While she’s a core part of your organic chemistry study group and you've spent plenty of time with her at the library and Sunrise Diner, she hasn't been on the volleyball roster.

When you did a "mental roster check" back in April 2025, you listed the team as:
*   **Hana** (Captain)
*   **Sienna** (Setter)
*   **Reggie** (Middle Blocker)
*   **Nadia** and **Marcus** (who have also stepped up in recent games)
*   **Coach Tran**

Beatrice has mostly stayed focused on her pre-med track, her color-coded notes, and her cat, Molecule. The closest she got to your volleyball world was when Nico tried to prank her by saying an orgo exam had been moved up right as playoffs were starting.

question: Was Professor Huang the one who taught my film class junior year?
expected: No. Professor Huang is the Organic Chemistry professor. The film class was taught by a different, 'super chill' professor whose name is not mentioned.
response:
No, Professor Huang was your Organic Chemistry professor. You took a film class as a gen ed during the spring of your sophomore year (2025), but you haven't mentioned who the professor was—only that it was the "easiest A" of your life and the final was just watching a movie and writing about it.

By the time you started junior year in the fall of 2025, you actually switched to a new professor for Orgo (Dr. Patel) because you were finished with Huang's classes.

question: Did I ever get a chance to meet Malik’s parents during the time he and Zara were together?
expected: No. There is no mention of meeting Malik's parents. You met Kwame's parents during Thanksgiving, but your interaction with Malik was limited to seeing him at parties and during his 'situationship' with Zara.
response:
No, you never mentioned meeting Malik’s parents. 

During the time he and Zara were together (roughly September to October 2024), your interactions with him were mostly at Theo’s house or through Zara. You mentioned hanging out with them at Theo's place on September 26, 2024, where you played Mario Kart and Malik was "salty" about you winning twice, but his family never came up.

By contrast, you've been much more involved with the families of people Zara dated later—you know that Andre (her current boyfriend) met her family during the 2024 holidays, and you've even spent Thanksgiving with your roommate Kwame's family. But for Malik, the closest you got to "family" talk was Zara texting you in November 2024 that she saw him at a coffee shop with someone else after they broke up.
